input_dict.question: The musician also known as "Braddah Iz"  is a member of what Hawaiian musical group?
input_dict.source_summary: Israel Kamakawiwoʻole is a member of the Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau. The Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau were formed in 1976 on Oahu and included him as one of the founding members.
input_dict.target_summary: Israel Kaʻanoʻi Kamakawiwoʻole (also called "Braddah Iz") — identified in summary_1 as a Native Hawaiian musician — is attested in the hop2 retrieval as one of the members/founding members of The Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau. The Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau are described in the retrieved passage as a Hawaiian musical group formed in 1976 on the island of Oahu by Jerome Koko, Louis "Moon" Kauakahi, Skippy Kamakawiwo'ole, Israel Kamakawiwoʻole, and Sam Gray. Retained facts for final answer: Israel Kamakawiwoʻole's alias "Braddah Iz" and his membership/founding membership in The Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau (group name, formation year and island).
input_dict.candidate_summary: Israel Kamakawiwoʻole is a member of the Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau. Israel Kamakawiwoʻole was a founding member of the Mākaha Sons of Ni'ihau, a Hawaiian musical group formed in 1976.
